mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic slowdown, prompting risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit expansion indicates conflicting demand signals; when housing starts rise while consumer credit stagnates, it reflects a structural shift toward durable goods that supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive 60-day forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the combined signal explains less than 2% of return variance over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a high-inflation environment indicates supply-side capacity constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-INDPRO periods, or the rolling 1-year correlation between INDPRO changes and 20d SPX returns exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real GDP acts as a baseline momentum driver for corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as institutional flows chase confirmed growth.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative GDPC1 growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average corporate return on invested capital, triggering deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending without triggering inflationary wage spirals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.2 over a 3-year test, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, a sharp rebound in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite rapidly returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts rebounds fail to precede SPX gains in > 60% of post-shock episodes, or the 5-day forward return differential between rebound and non-rebound months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
